mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, increasing the present value of future corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positively correlated with declining FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 7 percent signals deficient aggregate demand and weak corporate revenue growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising consumer prices and falling payroll employment create a stagflationary compression on profit margins and real consumption, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month while PAYEMS decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and falling PAYEMS fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered months, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power in regression (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid retail sales contraction, declining industrial production amplifies the negative demand shock and causes sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as market participants downgrade near-term earnings.
failure_condition: During retail sales contraction regimes, declining INDPRO does not coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of instances, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is not significantly lower than unconditional (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in industrial production indicates improving corporate operating leverage and revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 quarterly returns as earnings surprises skew upward.
failure_condition: Positive monthly changes in INDPRO do not coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25 percent traps monetary policy at the effective lower bound, removing the central bank put and amplifying risk-aversion, producing negative S&P 500 weekly returns as volatility rises.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of weeks over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment combined with falling retail sales signals a profit-squeeze environment where labor costs absorb revenue without top-line growth, generating negative S&P 500 monthly returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month while RSAFS decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising PAYEMS and falling RSAFS fails to predict negative 20-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 55%, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in logistic regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts below 1,400 thousand units signals weakening construction activity and durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns via downstream industrial and credit channel contraction.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1,400, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months over a 3-year rolling window.
